     672                 :           0 : void Temperature::createConcentricCoreNodes() {
     673         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto core = _magnetic.get_core();
     674         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto columns = core.get_columns();
     675         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto processedDesc = core.get_processed_description();
     676                 :             :     
     677         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (columns.empty()) {
     678                 :             :         throw std::runtime_error("Temperature::createConcentricCoreNodes: Core has no columns. "
     679         [ #  # ]:           0 :                                  "Cannot build thermal model without core column geometry.");
     680                 :             :     }
     681         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (!processedDesc) {
     682                 :             :         throw std::runtime_error("Temperature::createConcentricCoreNodes: Core is missing processed description. "
     683         [ #  # ]:           0 :                                  "Cannot build thermal model without core dimensions.");
     684                 :             :     }
     685                 :             :     
     686         [ #  # ]:           0 :     double coreK = getCoreThermalConductivity();
     687                 :           0 :     double coreWidth = processedDesc->get_width();
     688                 :           0 :     double coreHeight = processedDesc->get_height();
     689                 :           0 :     double coreDepth = processedDesc->get_depth();
     690                 :             :     
     691                 :             :     // Get gaps for each column
     692         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto gaps = core.get_functional_description().get_gapping();
     693                 :             :     
     694                 :             :     // Find main (central) column and right lateral column
     695   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     auto mainColumn = core.find_closest_column_by_coordinates({0, 0, 0});
     696                 :             :     auto rightColumn = core.find_closest_column_by_coordinates(
     697   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         std::vector<double>({coreWidth / 2, 0, -coreDepth / 2}));
     698                 :             :     
     699                 :             :     // Helper to count gaps in a column
     700                 :             :     
     701                 :             : 
     702                 :             :     // =========================================================================
     703                 :             :     // Volume-proportional loss distribution (replaces hardcoded 40/20/10/10)
     704                 :             :     // =========================================================================
     705   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     auto corePiece = CorePiece::factory(core.resolve_shape());
     706         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto lossFractions = corePiece->calculate_core_loss_fractions();
     707                 :             : 
     708                 :           0 :     double centralColumnLosses = _config.coreLosses * lossFractions.centralColumn;
     709                 :           0 :     double lateralColumnLosses = _config.coreLosses * lossFractions.lateralColumn;
     710                 :           0 :     double topYokeLosses       = _config.coreLosses * lossFractions.topYoke;
     711                 :           0 :     double bottomYokeLosses    = _config.coreLosses * lossFractions.bottomYoke;
     712                 :             : 
     713                 :             :     // Core loss distribution calculated
     714                 :             :     // =========================================================================
     715                 :             : 
     716                 :             :     // =========================================================================
     717                 :             :     // IMP-NEW-12: HALF-CORE SYMMETRY MODEL
     718                 :             :     // =========================================================================
     719                 :             :     // This model uses HALF the core depth for concentric cores (left-right symmetry).
     720                 :             :     // - Node volumes are halved -> power dissipation per node is correct (same W/m3)
     721                 :             :     // - Surface areas are halved -> convection area represents one half only
     722                 :             :     // - The symmetry correction is applied by doubling all convection surface areas
     723                 :             :     //   to account for the other symmetric half
     724                 :             :     // =========================================================================
     725                 :             :     // Create central column node(s) - using HALF depth for symmetry (left half only)
     726                 :           0 :     double halfDepth = mainColumn.get_depth() / 2.0;  // Model half the core
     727                 :             :     // Always create a single central column node with standard name for painter compatibility
     728                 :             :     // (Chunked nodes would require painter support for matching)
     729                 :           0 :     ThermalNetworkNode node;
     730                 :           0 :     node.part = ThermalNodePartType::CORE_CENTRAL_COLUMN;
     731         [ #  # ]:           0 :     node.name = "Core_Column_0";
     732                 :           0 :     node.temperature = _config.ambientTemperature;
     733                 :           0 :     node.powerDissipation = centralColumnLosses; // Volume-proportional
     734         [ #  # ]:           0 :     node.physicalCoordinates = {0, 0, 0};
     735         [ #  # ]:           0 :     node.initializeConcentricCoreQuadrants(mainColumn.get_width(), 
     736                 :           0 :                                             mainColumn.get_height(), halfDepth, coreK);
     737         [ #  # ]:           0 :     _nodes.push_back(node);
     738                 :             :

    1966                 :             :     // NOTE: O(N^2) complexity over turn nodes. For designs with >1000 turns,

    1972                 :             :         
    1973         [ #  # ]:           0 :         for (size_t j = i + 1; j < turnNodeIndices.size(); j++) {

    1976                 :             :             
    1977                 :           0 :             double dx = node1.physicalCoordinates[0] - node2.physicalCoordinates[0];

    1979                 :           0 :             double centerDistance = std::sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy);

    1986                 :             :             // For rectangular wires, calculate surface distance based on relative orientation

    1989         [ #  # ]:           0 :             if (centerDistance < 1e-9) {
    1990                 :           0 :                 surfaceDistance = 0;

    2004                 :             :             
    2005                 :             :             // Determine primary direction of connection

    2008         [ #  # ]:           0 :             if (moreHorizontal) {
    2009                 :             :                 // Horizontal connection - use RADIAL faces
    2010         [ #  # ]:           0 :                 if (dx > 0) {
    2011                 :             :                     // node1 is to the right of node2

    2014                 :             :                 } else {
    2015                 :             :                     // node1 is to the left of node2

    2020                 :             :                 // Vertical connection - use TANGENTIAL faces

    2078                 :             :

    2131                 :             :

    2661                 :             :     // Find all insulation layer nodes

    2730                 :             :                 }

    2757                 :             :                     // Distance to RIGHT insulation (insulation's left surface)

    2843                 :             :     
    2844                 :             :     // ============================================================================
    2845                 :             :     // Toroidal Turn-to-Insulation Connections (Radial Proximity + Angular Matching)
    2846                 :             :     // ============================================================================
    2847                 :             :     // For each turn node:
    2848                 :             :     // 1. Check both radial sides (inner and outer surface) against all insulation layers
    2849                 :             :     // 2. If turn surface is close to layer surface (within 15% of wire radius), mark for connection
    2850                 :             :     // 3. Find the closest insulation node by angle for each matching layer
    2851                 :             :     // 4. Choose the quadrant combination (RI-RO or RO-RI) with closest limit coordinates
    2852                 :           0 :     size_t connectionCount = 0;
    2853   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     if (_isToroidal && !insulationNodeIndices.empty()) {
                   #  # ]
    2854                 :             :         // Get core dimensions for inner/outer region detection
    2855         [ #  # ]:           0 :         auto core = _magnetic.get_core();
    2856   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         auto coreDims = flatten_dimensions(core.resolve_shape().get_dimensions().value());
             #  #  #  # ]

    3482                 :             :

    3800                 :           0 : void Temperature::createPlanarConvectionConnections(size_t ambientIdx, double h_conv) {
    3801                 :             :         // ============================================================================
    3802                 :             :         // Planar case: Turn quadrants connect to closest FR4 layer (conduction),

    3933                 :             :                     // No distance limit — find the nearest FR4 quadrant

    3961                 :           0 :                                 minDist = dist;

    3964                 :             :                             }
    3965                 :             :                         }

    3984                 :             :                 }
    3985                 :             :             } // end quadrant loop

    3987                 :             : 
    3988                 :             :         // 5. Connect FR4 insulation layer quadrants to ambient (for heat dissipation)
    3989                 :             :         // Only topmost and bottommost FR4 layers are exposed to ambient
    3990                 :             :         // Surface area is reduced by copper coverage from top/bottom turns
    3991                 :             : 
    3992                 :             :         // Find topmost and bottommost FR4 layers by Y-coordinate
    3993                 :           0 :         double fr4MaxY = -1e9;

    3996                 :           0 :             double fr4Y = _nodes[fr4Idx].physicalCoordinates[1];

    4000                 :             : 
    4001                 :             :         // Calculate copper coverage area on top and bottom surfaces

    4010                 :             :             // The copper area is the turn's cross-sectional area in the XZ plane
    4011   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :             if (_nodes[turnIdx].dimensions.width > 0 && _nodes[turnIdx].dimensions.height > 0) {

    4015                 :             :

    4022                 :             : 
    4023                 :           0 :         double fr4Tolerance = 1e-5;  // Same small tolerance to detect distinct layers

    4030                 :             :

    4050                 :           0 :                     r.area = effectiveArea;

    4054                 :             :             }
    4055                 :             :
